ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-25-2017 Off Calendar

Motion For Court Judgment
Off Calendar Sep-19-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Gail Dekreon; Clerk: J. Bertelsen; Reporter: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840.

Scott Dunning, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, Nissim Ninio.
No appearance by defaulted defendant Gibson Canites.
Default of Defendant has been duly entered.
Witness Nissim Ninio sworn and examined on behalf of Plaintiff.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant as follows:

Damages: $27,000.00
Pre-Judgment Interest: $6364.00
Attorney's Fees: $5480.00
Costs: $1882.00
Total Judgment in the amount of $40,726.00
Judgment signed in open court.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reinstate Default And Default Judgment
Off Calendar May-22-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. SCOTT JODAN OF THE DUNNING LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-925-362-3598 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Nissim Ninio's motion to reinstate default and default judgment is granted in part. Defendant Gibson Canites must file a response to the complaint no later June 12, 2017. If Mr. Canites does not do so and a copy of this order is served by mail on Mr. Canites no later than May 23, 2017, then plaintiff may seek entry of Mr. Canites' default and a default judgment. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/hk

Off Calendar Mar-04-2016 Continued To May-04-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment; Memo Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Defendant
Off Calendar Mar-15-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT GIBSON CANITES's Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment is granted. Defendant's declaration that he had no knowledge of this case until he received notice of the default is sufficient to set aside the default and default judgment. Moreover, the strong public policy favoring resolution of cases on their merits supports the granting of the motion. Plaintiff's request for costs is denied because there is no basis to conclude that Mr. Canites acted other than responsibly. The prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
